info.title: specsync
info.description: SpecSync for Azure DevOps is a synchronization tool that synchronizes Gherkin feature files with Azure DevOps (TFS/VSTS). Please find more information and detailed documentation at https://speclink.me/specsync.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Synchronization tool for integrating BDD scenarios with Azure DevOps

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: --projectUrl
options.description: The project URL of the Azure DevOps project. [default: interactive prompt]

options.name: --config
options.description: The full path or the folder of the configuration file being created. [default: 'specsync.json' file in the current folder]

options.name: --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint
options.description: The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]

options.name: --verbose
options.description: If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]

options.name: --zeroExitCodeForWarnings
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]

options.name: --forceExitCode
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Azure DevOps pipeline. [default: not specified]

options.name: --treatWarningsAsErrors
options.description: If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]

options.name: --diag
options.description: Enable special diagnostic categories to be logged. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ','. [default: no category]

options.name: --debug
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will start with the debugger attached. [default: not specified]

options.name: --config
options.description: The full path or the folder of the configuration file to process. [default: 'specsync.json' file in the current folder]

options.name: --verbose
options.description: If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]

options.name: --zeroExitCodeForWarnings
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]

options.name: --forceExitCode
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Azure DevOps pipeline. [default: not specified]

options.name: --treatWarningsAsErrors
options.description: If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]

options.name: --diag
options.description: Enable special diagnostic categories to be logged. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ','. [default: no category]

options.name: --debug
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will start with the debugger attached. [default: not specified]

commands.name: push
commands.description: Push changes of the scenarios on the local repository to the Azure DevOps server. This by default includes linking of new scenarios to new test cases (link) and updating test cases of linked scenarios (update).

options.name: --linkOnly
options.description: If specified, the command will link new scenarios to new Test Cases only and the existing Test Cases will not be updated. [default: not specified]

options.name: --disableLocalChanges
options.description: If specified, only those changes will be performed that do not need any change in the local feature file. Linking new scenarios or pulling changes from Azure DevOps are skipped. [default: not specified]

options.name: --force
options.description: If specified, SpecSync update test cases even if there is no local change and the test case was not modified remotely. [default: not specified]

options.name: --filter
options.description: A local test case condition of local test cases that should be included in the current operation (e.g. '@current_sprint and @done' or '$name = "My scenario"'). See https://speclink.me/specsync-conditions for details. [default: not filtered]

options.name: --sourceFileFilter
options.description: An expression of source file patterns that should be included in the current operation (e.g. 'Folder1/**/*.feature'). [default: not filtered by source file]

options.name: --user
options.description: The Azure DevOps user name or '?' to force interactive prompt. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

options.name: --password
options.description: The password for the Azure DevOps user. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

options.name: --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint
options.description: The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]

options.name: --dryRun
options.description: If specified, the command will be performed, but no actual change is made either to Azure DevOps or to the local test files (feature files). [default: not specified]

options.name: --license
options.description: The license key or the path to the license file (can be relative to the project folder). [default: use from config file or 'specsync.lic']

options.name: --disableStats
options.description: If specified, SpecSync will not collect anonymous error diagnostics and statistics. [default: not specified]

options.name: --log
options.description: If specified, the output will also be saved to a log file. [default: no log file written]

options.name: --configOverride
options.description: Can be used to override configuration file settings. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. See https://speclink.me/specsync-configoverride for details.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]

options.name: --zeroExitCodeForWarnings
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]

options.name: --forceExitCode
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Azure DevOps pipeline. [default: not specified]

options.name: --treatWarningsAsErrors
options.description: If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]

options.name: --diag
options.description: Enable special diagnostic categories to be logged. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ','. [default: no category]

options.name: --debug
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will start with the debugger attached. [default: not specified]

arguments.name: CONFIG_FILE
arguments.description: The path of the SpecSync for Azure DevOps configuration file, absolute path or relative to the current folder, e.g. 'MyProject.Specs\specsync4tfs.json'. [default: specsync.json]

commands.name: pull
commands.description: Pull changes from Azure DevOps server to the local repository. This by default includes creation of new scenarios from unlinked test cases (create) and changing scenarios of linked test cases (change).

options.name: --createOnly
options.description: If specified, the command will create new scenarios for the unlinked Test Cases only and the existing scenarios will not be modified. This setting automatically enables the 'enableCreatingNewLocalTestCases' pull setting. [default: not specified]

options.name: --disableLocalChanges
options.description: If specified, only those changes will be performed that do not need any change in the local feature file. Linking new scenarios or pulling changes from Azure DevOps are skipped. [default: not specified]

options.name: --force
options.description: If specified, SpecSync update test cases even if there is no local change and the test case was not modified remotely. [default: not specified]

options.name: --filter
options.description: A local test case condition of local test cases that should be included in the current operation (e.g. '@current_sprint and @done' or '$name = "My scenario"'). See https://speclink.me/specsync-conditions for details. [default: not filtered]

options.name: --sourceFileFilter
options.description: An expression of source file patterns that should be included in the current operation (e.g. 'Folder1/**/*.feature'). [default: not filtered by source file]

options.name: --user
options.description: The Azure DevOps user name or '?' to force interactive prompt. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

options.name: --password
options.description: The password for the Azure DevOps user. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

options.name: --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint
options.description: The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]

options.name: --dryRun
options.description: If specified, the command will be performed, but no actual change is made either to Azure DevOps or to the local test files (feature files). [default: not specified]

options.name: --license
options.description: The license key or the path to the license file (can be relative to the project folder). [default: use from config file or 'specsync.lic']

options.name: --disableStats
options.description: If specified, SpecSync will not collect anonymous error diagnostics and statistics. [default: not specified]

options.name: --log
options.description: If specified, the output will also be saved to a log file. [default: no log file written]

options.name: --configOverride
options.description: Can be used to override configuration file settings. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. See https://speclink.me/specsync-configoverride for details.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]

options.name: --zeroExitCodeForWarnings
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]

options.name: --forceExitCode
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Azure DevOps pipeline. [default: not specified]

options.name: --treatWarningsAsErrors
options.description: If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]

options.name: --diag
options.description: Enable special diagnostic categories to be logged. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ','. [default: no category]

options.name: --debug
options.description: If specified, the command line tool will start with the debugger attached. [default: not specified]

arguments.name: CONFIG_FILE
arguments.description: The path of the SpecSync for Azure DevOps configuration file, absolute path or relative to the current folder, e.g. 'MyProject.Specs\specsync4tfs.json'. [default: specsync.json]

commands.name: publish-test-results
commands.description: Publish local test results to Azure DevOps server.

options.name: --testResult
options.description: The file path of the test result (.trx, .xml or .json) file to publish. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. [default: use from config file]

options.name: --testResultFormat
options.description: The file format of the file to publish (use '?' to list possible formats). [default: use from config file or detect automatically]

options.name: --attachFile
options.description: Semicolon separated list of file paths that should be attached to the test run additionally. (e.g. 'error1.png;error2.log') This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. [default: only the test result file is attached]

options.name: --runName
options.description: The name of the test run to be created. Can also be used as '--testRunSetting "name=value"'. [default: get from test result file]

options.name: --runComment
options.description: The comment or description field of the test run to be created. Can also be used as '--testRunSetting "comment=value"'. [default: get from test result file]

options.name: --testResultComment
options.description: The comment added to the individual test results within the created test run. Can also be used as '--testResultSetting "comment=value"'. [default: not specified]

options.name: --testRunSetting
options.description: Can be used to specify additional settings for the test run to be created. For Azure DevOps you can specify 'name', 'comment', 'runType', 'buildId', 'buildNumber', 'buildFlavor', 'buildPlatform'. This option can be used multiple times using the 'setting=value' format, e.g. --testRunSetting "comment=Manual run". This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'.

options.name: --testResultSetting
options.description: Can be used to specify additional settings for the individual test results. For Azure DevOps test results you can specify 'comment'. This option can be used multiple times using the 'setting=value' format, e.g. --testResultSetting "comment=Manual run". This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'.

options.name: --testConfiguration
options.description: The Azure DevOps test configuration name or ID to publish the results for. For specifying an ID, use '#1234' format. [default: use from config file]

options.name: --disablePipelineAssociation
options.description: If specified, the published test results will not be associated to the build or release pipeline. This is useful if the Azure DevOps project of the build is different to the project of the Test Cases. See https://speclink.me/specsync-pipeline-not-found for details. [default: not specified]

options.name: --testSuite
options.description: A test suite name or ID to publish the test results to. For specifying an ID, use '#1234' format. (e.g. 'My Suite' or '#1234') [default: use from config file]

options.name: --testPlan
options.description: The name or the ID of the test plan to search the test suite in. For specifying an ID, use '#1234' format. (e.g. 'My Plan' or '#1234') [default: all test plans are scanned through]

options.name: --hierarchy
options.description: A name of a hierarchy to publish the test results to. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'.

options.name: --buildId
options.description: Deprecated. Please use '--testRunSetting buildId=<value>' instead [default: detect from current build]

options.name: --buildNumber
options.description: Deprecated. Please use '--testRunSetting buildNumber=<value>' instead [default: detect from current build]

options.name: --buildFlavor
options.description: Deprecated. Please use '--testRunSetting buildFlavor=<value>' instead [default: detect from current build]

options.name: --buildPlatform
options.description: Deprecated. Please use '--testRunSetting buildFlavor=<value>' instead [default: detect from current build]

options.name: --testPlanId
options.description: Deprecated. Please use '--testPlan' instead. [default: all test plans are scanned through]

options.name: --filter
options.description: A local test case condition of local test cases that should be included in the current operation (e.g. '@current_sprint and @done' or '$name = "My scenario"'). See https://speclink.me/specsync-conditions for details. [default: not filtered]

options.name: --sourceFileFilter
options.description: An expression of source file patterns that should be included in the current operation (e.g. 'Folder1/**/*.feature'). [default: not filtered by source file]

options.name: --user
options.description: The Azure DevOps user name or '?' to force interactive prompt. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

options.name: --password
options.description: The password for the Azure DevOps user. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

options.description: The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]

commands.name: re-link
commands.description: Re-link scenarios (local test cases) to cloned Test Cases in Azure DevOps.

options.name: --cloneOpId
options.description: The Id (e.g. '11') of the Clone Operation the scenarios should be re-linked for. [default: detect from Test Cases]

options.name: --workItemClonesFile
options.description: A path of a CSV file that contains additional Work Item clones to be processed in link tags. The file must have a 'source' and a 'target' column. [default: no additional Work Item clones provided]

options.name: --user
options.description: The Azure DevOps user name or '?' to force interactive prompt. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

options.name: --password
options.description: The password for the Azure DevOps user. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

options.name: --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint
options.description: The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]
